how to remove space in between numbers in excel

To remove spaces in between numbers in Excel, you can use the following steps:

1. Highlight the cells that contain the numbers or range of cells that you want to remove spaces from.
2. Click on the "Find & Select" button in the "Editing" section of the "Home" tab on the Ribbon, and select "Replace".
3. In the "Find what" field, press the spacebar once to add a single blank space.
4. Leave the "Replace with" field blank. This will remove any space found in the cell without adding any new character.
5. Click on "Replace All" to remove every space in the selected cells.

This will remove all spaces between numbers in the selected cells in Excel.

To remove "94" from the beginning of all the numbers, you can use the following steps in Excel:

1. Highlight the cells with the numbers you want to edit.
2. Press "Ctrl+H" to open the Find and Replace window.
3. In the "Find What" field, type "94 ".
4. Leave the "Replace With" field blank.
5. Click "Replace All" to remove all occurrences of "94 " at the beginning of the numbers.

After following these steps, your numbers will appear without "94" at the beginning:

To add a plus sign (+) in front of all the numbers, you can use the following steps in Excel:

1. Insert a new column to the left of the column containing the numbers.
2. In the first row of the new column, type "+" in the cell.
3. Copy the "+" symbol.
4. Select the entire new column. Use Ctrl + Shift + Down Arrow keys to select the entire column.
5. Press Ctrl + V to paste the copied "+" symbol. All cells in the column should contain the "+" symbol.
6. Highlight both columns, including the "+" symbol.
7. Press Ctrl + C to copy the entire selection.
8. Right-click on the original column containing the numbers and select "Paste Special" from the pop-up menu.
9. In the "Paste Special" window, select "Values" and click OK.
10. Once the values have been pasted, the plus sign will appear in front of all numbers.

You can now delete the new column with the "+" symbols if you no longer need it. Alternatively, you can use a custom number format to add the plus sign without inserting a new column. In the "Custom" number format, you can use the following format code: `+0############`.
